description: Network exploration tool and security/port scanner.

arguments.name: Input from List
arguments.description: Read targets from a file instead of typing them on the command line. One host or network per line.

arguments.name: Random Targets
arguments.description: Scan a given number of randomly chosen targets from the internet.

arguments.name: Exclude Hosts
arguments.description: Skip these hosts or networks during the scan.

arguments.name: Exclude File
arguments.description: Read a list of hosts to exclude from a file.

arguments.name: List Scan
arguments.description: List targets without scanning them. Useful for verifying which hosts would be scanned.

arguments.name: Ping Scan
arguments.description: Ping scan only -- check which hosts are online without scanning their ports.

arguments.name: Skip Host Discovery
arguments.description: Skip host discovery and treat all targets as online. Useful when ICMP ping is blocked.

arguments.name: TCP SYN Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts by sending a TCP SYN packet to the specified ports.

arguments.name: TCP ACK Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts by sending a TCP ACK packet to the specified ports.

arguments.name: UDP Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts by sending a UDP packet to the specified ports.

arguments.name: SCTP Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts by sending an SCTP INIT packet to the specified ports.

arguments.name: ICMP Echo Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts using ICMP echo requests (standard ping).

arguments.name: ICMP Timestamp Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts using ICMP timestamp requests. Useful when echo requests are blocked.

arguments.name: ICMP Netmask Discovery
arguments.description: Discover hosts using ICMP netmask requests. Another alternative when echo requests are blocked.

arguments.name: IP Protocol Ping
arguments.description: Discover hosts by sending IP packets with the specified protocol numbers.

arguments.name: No DNS Resolution
arguments.description: Skip DNS lookups. Makes the scan faster when you don't need hostnames.

arguments.name: Always Resolve DNS
arguments.description: Always resolve hostnames to DNS, even for offline hosts.

arguments.name: DNS Servers
arguments.description: Use specific DNS servers for hostname lookups instead of your system defaults.

arguments.name: System DNS
arguments.description: Use your operating system's DNS resolver instead of nmap's built-in one.

arguments.name: Traceroute
arguments.description: Show the network path (hops) to each target host.

arguments.name: TCP SYN Scan
arguments.description: SYN scan -- fast stealth scan that doesn't complete TCP connections. Requires elevated privileges.

arguments.name: TCP Connect Scan
arguments.description: TCP connect scan -- completes the full TCP handshake. Slower but doesn't need root.

arguments.name: TCP ACK Scan
arguments.description: Send ACK packets to determine which ports are filtered by a firewall. Does not find open ports.

arguments.name: TCP Window Scan
arguments.description: Like ACK scan but uses TCP window size to tell open from closed ports on some systems.

arguments.name: TCP Maimon Scan
arguments.description: Send FIN/ACK packets to probe ports. Works against some BSD-based systems.

arguments.name: UDP Scan
arguments.description: UDP scan -- scans UDP ports. Much slower than TCP scans. Can be combined with a TCP scan type.

arguments.name: TCP Null Scan
arguments.description: Send TCP packets with no flags set. Can slip past some firewalls that only watch for SYN packets.

arguments.name: TCP FIN Scan
arguments.description: Send TCP packets with only the FIN flag. Can bypass some firewalls that only look for SYN packets.

arguments.name: TCP Xmas Scan
arguments.description: Send TCP packets with FIN, PSH, and URG flags set. Another way to sneak past certain firewalls.

arguments.name: Custom Scan Flags
arguments.description: Build a custom TCP scan by choosing exactly which TCP flags to set.

arguments.name: Idle Scan
arguments.description: Scan through an idle 'zombie' host so the target never sees your real IP address.

arguments.name: SCTP INIT Scan
arguments.description: Scan SCTP ports by sending an INIT packet. Similar to TCP SYN scan but for the SCTP protocol.

arguments.name: SCTP COOKIE-ECHO Scan
arguments.description: Scan SCTP ports using COOKIE-ECHO packets. More stealthy than INIT scan but less reliable.

arguments.name: IP Protocol Scan
arguments.description: Find which IP protocols (TCP, UDP, ICMP, etc.) the target supports, rather than scanning ports.

arguments.name: FTP Bounce Scan
arguments.description: Scan through a misconfigured FTP server to hide your identity. Rarely works on modern servers.

arguments.name: Port Specification
arguments.description: Port range to scan. Examples: '80' (single port), '1-1000' (range), '80,443,8080' (list), '-' (all 65535 ports).

arguments.name: Exclude Ports
arguments.description: Skip these ports during the scan.

arguments.name: Fast Mode
arguments.description: Scan only the 100 most common ports instead of the default 1,000.

arguments.name: Sequential Ports
arguments.description: Scan ports in order (1, 2, 3...) instead of randomizing. Useful for predictable progress.

arguments.name: Top Ports
arguments.description: Scan only the N most commonly used ports.

arguments.name: Port Ratio
arguments.description: Scan ports that are more common than the given ratio (between 0 and 1).

arguments.name: Service Version Detection
arguments.description: Probe open ports to determine what service and version is running.

arguments.name: Version Intensity
arguments.description: Control how hard nmap tries to identify services. 0 = light (fast), 9 = try everything (slow).

arguments.name: Version Light
arguments.description: Use only the most likely probes for version detection (intensity 2). Faster but less accurate.

arguments.name: Version All
arguments.description: Try every probe for version detection (intensity 9). Most thorough but slowest.

arguments.name: Version Trace
arguments.description: Show detailed version scan activity for debugging purposes.

arguments.name: Default Scripts
arguments.description: Run default NSE scripts. Same as --script=default.

arguments.name: Script
arguments.description: Run NSE scripts against targets. Use script categories (like 'vuln' or 'safe') or specific script names.

arguments.name: Script Arguments
arguments.description: Pass arguments to NSE scripts as key=value pairs separated by commas.

arguments.name: Script Arguments File
arguments.description: Load NSE script arguments from a file instead of typing them on the command line.

arguments.name: Script Trace
arguments.description: Show all network data sent and received by scripts. Useful for debugging script issues.

arguments.name: Script Update DB
arguments.description: Rebuild the NSE script database. Run this after adding or removing scripts.

arguments.name: Script Help
arguments.description: Show documentation for the specified scripts or script categories.

arguments.name: OS Detection
arguments.description: Attempt to detect the target's operating system. Requires elevated privileges.

arguments.name: OS Scan Limit
arguments.description: Only attempt OS detection on hosts that have at least one open and one closed port.

arguments.name: OS Scan Guess
arguments.description: Make more aggressive guesses about the target OS when an exact match isn't found.

arguments.name: Timing Template
arguments.description: Scan speed. 0 = paranoid/slowest, 3 = normal (default), 5 = insane/fastest. Higher values are louder on the network.

arguments.name: Min Host Group
arguments.description: Minimum number of hosts to scan at the same time.

arguments.name: Max Host Group
arguments.description: Maximum number of hosts to scan at the same time.

arguments.name: Min Parallelism
arguments.description: Minimum number of probes to send in parallel.

arguments.name: Max Parallelism
arguments.description: Maximum number of probes to send in parallel. Lower values reduce network load.

arguments.name: Min RTT Timeout
arguments.description: Minimum time to wait for a probe response. Prevents nmap from giving up too quickly on slow networks.

arguments.name: Max RTT Timeout
arguments.description: Maximum time to wait for a probe response before giving up.

arguments.name: Initial RTT Timeout
arguments.description: Starting timeout value for the first probe. Nmap adjusts this automatically as it learns the network speed.

arguments.name: Max Retries
arguments.description: Maximum number of times to resend a probe that got no response.

arguments.name: Host Timeout
arguments.description: Give up on a host if the scan takes longer than this. Useful for skipping very slow hosts.

arguments.name: Scan Delay
arguments.description: Wait at least this long between each probe. Useful for rate-limited or sensitive targets.

arguments.name: Max Scan Delay
arguments.description: Maximum delay between probes, even when nmap wants to slow down further.

arguments.name: Min Rate
arguments.description: Send at least this many packets per second, regardless of network conditions.

arguments.name: Max Rate
arguments.description: Send no more than this many packets per second. Useful for avoiding network disruption.

arguments.name: Fragment Packets
arguments.description: Split packets into tiny fragments to evade some firewalls and intrusion detection systems.

arguments.name: MTU
arguments.description: Fragment packets to a specific size (must be a multiple of 8). Alternative to -f with more control.

arguments.name: Decoys
arguments.description: Send fake scans from decoy IP addresses alongside yours to hide your real address.

arguments.name: Spoof Source Address
arguments.description: Fake the source IP address on outgoing packets. You won't see responses unless you also set -e.

arguments.name: Interface
arguments.description: Send packets through a specific network interface (e.g., eth0 or wlan0).

arguments.name: Source Port
arguments.description: Send packets from a specific source port number. Some firewalls allow traffic from certain ports like 53 or 80.

arguments.name: Proxies
arguments.description: Route scan traffic through HTTP or SOCKS4 proxy servers.

arguments.name: Data Hex
arguments.description: Append custom hex data to outgoing packets.

arguments.name: Data String
arguments.description: Append a custom text string to outgoing packets.

arguments.name: Data Length
arguments.description: Pad packets with random data to the given length. Helps disguise scan traffic.

arguments.name: IP Options
arguments.description: Set custom IP header options on outgoing packets. Advanced networking feature.

arguments.name: TTL
arguments.description: Set the time-to-live (hop limit) for outgoing packets.

arguments.name: Spoof MAC
arguments.description: Fake your MAC address. Use a specific address, a vendor name, or 0 for a random one.

arguments.name: Bad Checksum
arguments.description: Send packets with intentionally bad checksums. Useful for testing firewall/IDS behavior.

arguments.name: Normal Output
arguments.description: Save scan results in normal text format to a file.

arguments.name: XML Output
arguments.description: Save scan results in XML format to a file. Useful for importing into other tools.

arguments.name: Script Kiddie Output
arguments.description: Save scan results in 'script kiddie' format (leet-speak). Mostly a novelty.

arguments.name: Grepable Output
arguments.description: Save scan results in a grep-friendly format, with one host per line.

arguments.name: Output All Formats
arguments.description: Save scan results in normal, XML, and grepable formats at once using the given base filename.

arguments.name: Verbosity
arguments.description: Increase output verbosity. Can be stacked (-v -v) for more detail.

arguments.name: Debugging
arguments.description: Show debugging output. Can be stacked (-d -d) for even more detail.

arguments.name: Reason
arguments.description: Show why each port is reported as open, closed, or filtered.

arguments.name: Open Ports Only
arguments.description: Only show ports that are open (or possibly open). Hides closed and filtered ports.

arguments.name: Packet Trace
arguments.description: Show every packet sent and received. Very verbose, mainly for debugging.

arguments.name: Interface List
arguments.description: Print your network interfaces and routing table, then exit. Useful for troubleshooting.

arguments.name: Append Output
arguments.description: Add results to the end of output files instead of overwriting them.

arguments.name: Resume Scan
arguments.description: Continue a previously interrupted scan from where it left off.

arguments.name: Non-Interactive
arguments.description: Disable keyboard shortcuts during the scan. Useful when running nmap in scripts.

arguments.name: Stylesheet
arguments.description: Apply an XSL stylesheet to transform XML output into HTML for viewing in a browser.

arguments.name: Web XML Stylesheet
arguments.description: Use the default stylesheet from nmap.org so XML output can be viewed as HTML in any browser.

arguments.name: No Stylesheet
arguments.description: Don't include any XSL stylesheet reference in XML output.

arguments.name: IPv6
arguments.description: Scan using IPv6 instead of IPv4.

arguments.name: Aggressive Scan
arguments.description: Aggressive scan -- enables OS detection, version detection, script scanning, and traceroute all at once.

arguments.name: Data Directory
arguments.description: Tell nmap where to find its data files (service probes, scripts, etc.) instead of the default location.

arguments.name: Send Ethernet
arguments.description: Send packets at the raw ethernet layer instead of the IP layer.

arguments.name: Send IP
arguments.description: Send packets at the IP layer instead of the raw ethernet layer.

arguments.name: Privileged
arguments.description: Tell nmap to assume you have root/admin privileges, even if it can't detect them.

arguments.name: Unprivileged
arguments.description: Tell nmap to assume you don't have root/admin privileges. Only unprivileged scan types will be used.

arguments.name: Version
arguments.description: Print the nmap version number and exit.

arguments.name: Help
arguments.description: Show a summary of all nmap options and exit.

arguments.name: Target Specification
arguments.description: IP address, hostname, or CIDR range to scan. Examples: '192.168.1.1', 'scanme.nmap.org', '10.0.0.0/24'.
